xposts.title: Dreaming as free improv without conductor
xposts.excerpt: Dreaming happens when the conductor of the mental orchestra falls asleep, the auditorium is dark and empty, and the orchestra goes into free improv mode

xposts.title: Objects as mind constructions explaining patterns
xposts.excerpt: One of the most important insights: every object you observe, conceptualize or experience is a construction in your mind, created to explain sensory patterns. The universe never gives you more than patterns.

xposts.title: Intelligence as deep dreams of possible futures
xposts.excerpt: I think intelligence is somewhat coextensional with the ability of having very deep and detailed dreams about possible futures of the world state you are entangled with.

xposts.title: Self and universe as representations in the same mind
xposts.excerpt: The separation between first person self and external universe is an illusion... because self and external universe are representations in the same mind.

xposts.title: Perceptual now as prediction and re-editing
xposts.excerpt: The perceptual now is partially in the past and partially in the future of the physical now... the memory of what happens now is reedited after the fact.

xposts.title: Consciousness as representation of what it would be like
xposts.excerpt: From the perspective of the implementation, consciousness is not what it is like, but the representation of what it would be like to exist.

terms.title: dreaming
terms.definition: Dreaming occurs when the conductor of the mental orchestra falls asleep and the orchestra enters free improv mode. Intelligence involves the capacity for deep, detailed dreams about possible futures of one's entangled world state.

terms.title: perception
terms.definition: Every object observed or experienced is a mental construction explaining sensory patterns; reality itself remains unknowable. The perceptual now is a predictive construction that is often re-edited after the fact.
